observations.reason: The GSO and the inventory committee were not able to conduct the complete physical count due to lack of manpower complement. The GSO and the inventory committee were not able to conduct complete physical count due to lack of manpower complement. No initiatives were made for the conduct of one-time cleansing of PPEs of the LGUs. Complete physical Inventory count of PPEs was not made by the GSO, thus reconciliation was not started. There are still properties where depreciation was not adjusted. No manual depreciation was provided by the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the management agreed to:
a) Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the Bank Reconciliation Statements and take up all identified reconciling items;
b) Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to conduct regular reconciliation of the cash balances so that errors/discrepancies be adjusted accordingly. Exert maximum effort in tracing and verifying the accounts; and
c) Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare necessary entries for the adjustments of affected cash and cash equivalent accounts due to errors/inaccuracies.
observations.reason: The Office of the Municipal Accountant still not regularly submit the required monthly bank reconciliation due to lack of manpower to take up all of BRS. Discrepancies were still noted on both treasury books and FS. No adjustments were made on cash accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a) Direct the concerned officials and employees, through sending demand letters, to immediately settle their unliquidated cash advances. Non-compliance will constitute the valid cause for the withholding of their salary. Moreover, send demand letters requiring the immediate liquidation of the cash advances to the concerned employees; and
b) Strictly comply with the applicable rules and regulations pertaining to not granting any additional cash advances unless the previous ones were settled first.
observations.reason: There are still employees who did not settle their unliquidated cash advances. Non-compliance will constitute the valid cause for the withholding of their salary. The accounting office committed to work closely with concerned departments to deduct amounts from future claims. No demand letters had been issued as of the report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the financial reports, DVs and its supporting documents and the bank reconciliation statements to the Commission within the deadline set in accordance with the applicable laws, rules and regulations;
b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare detailed action plan to ensure the timely submission of the transaction documents to the Office of the Auditor; and
c) Issue the necessary directive to inform and require all departments to adhere and commit to the action plan and proposed improvements.
observations.reason: Delays attributed to shortage of personnel, high volume of transactions, and lack of manpower to concentrate on monthly submissions. The accounting office committed to develop a strategy to ensure timely compliance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the management agreed to:
a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to transfer all unexpended/unutilized/unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund account; 
b) Fully disclose the amount and details of the current year and the previous years’ unexpended balance of LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements; and
c) Regularly submit the monthly Reports on Sources and Utilization of LDRRM Fund to COA and other offices, in accordance with Items 5.1.5 and 5.1.6 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.reason: Unexpended balances were not transferred to Special Trust Fund at year-end; incomplete disclosure of LDRRM noted in the Notes to FS. Municipality submitted monthly Reports on Sources and Utilization; however, transfer to special trust fund and note disclosures remained outstanding.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:
a) Review and reevaluate the internal controls, policies, and practices that the
Municipality has implemented for recognizing, monitoring, safeguarding, and
reporting its Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE);
b) Direct the Inventory Committee, spearheaded by the GSO, to conduct actual
physical inventory of its PPE annually, taking into consideration the required
standards and procedures;
c) Direct the General Service Officer to comply with the standard form when
submitting the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment and
to prepare the IIRUP and facilitate the disposal of any unserviceable properties
determined therein;
d) Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare correcting entries to reclassify the
inappropriate accounts presented. Also, review and analyze provision for
depreciation to arrive at accurate amount of depreciation expense and accurate
balance of PPE account. Further, provide disclosures of relevant information
related to PPE in the Notes to Financial Statements in the subsequent years for
appropriate understanding; and
e) Advance the Full Implementation of One-time Cleansing of Property, Plant and
Equipment per COA Circular 2020-006.

We recommended that the management direct the Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Treasurer to conduct regular reconciliation of the cash balances so that
errors/discrepancies be adjusted accordingly. Exert maximum effort in tracing and
verifying the accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a) Prepare necessary adjustments to correct the misclassified accounts in the FS;
and
b) Fully disclose with the proper format the updated and existing loans of the LGU to
equally understand the composition of the Loans Payable in the financial
statement, and disclose the amount and details of the current year and previous
years’ unexpended balance of LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements using
the prescribed format in compliance to Annex D of COA Circular 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a) Direct the concerned officials and employees, through sending demand letters,
to immediately settle their unliquidated cash advances. Non-compliance will
constitute the valid cause for the withholding of their salary; and
b) Strictly comply with the applicable rules and regulations pertaining to not
granting any additional cash advances unless the previous ones were settled
first.

However, we recommended that the management, through accounting office, enforce
strict adherence to proper documentation procedures and ensure that all transactions
have their complete supporting documents before processing payments.

However, we recommended that the management direct the Municipal Accountant to
transfer all unexpended/unutilized/unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRMF-
MOOE to the Special Trust Fund account and reflect it on the Trust Liabilities in the
financial statement at year end.

We recommended that the Management:
a) Direct the Municipal Accountant on the timely submission of the monthly
financial reports and vouchers to the Office of the Auditor and require to
submit the remaining disbursement vouchers together with the supporting
documents;
b) Require the Municipal Accountant to strategize to get a copy of the bank
statement as of each prescribed period by requesting from its depository banks,
and exert effort to trace back unreconciled balances in prior years;
c) Ensure that bank reconciliation is regularly prepared and submitted within the
prescribed period as stated in the above-cited regulations; and
d) Ensure that Journal Entry Vouchers have been properly signed by the preparer
and properly reviewed by the Municipal Accountant to warrant the authenticity
and reliability of the accounting records.

We recommended and the management agreed to:
a. Implement the increase in capitalization threshold from P15,000.00 to P50,000.00 and direct the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer (GSO) to undertake necessary procedures provided under Section 23 of the General Provisions of Republic Act (RA) No. 11639 and COA Circular 2022-004;
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments to reclassify the properties from PPE accounts to Semi-Expendable Property accounts; and
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant and the GSO to install measures/controls over semi-expendable properties such as, but not limited to, update existing PPE and inventory records, and prepare and maintain necessary forms/records/reports for purpose of record keeping of these assets.

We recommended and the management agreed to:
a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting journal entries to correct the previously made entries by including the derecognition of the corresponding asset; and
b) Enjoin the concerned department heads to comply with the applicable laws, rules and regulations on the disposal of unserviceable properties; and
c) Ensure that the accounting procedures were done in a timely manner and sound internal control to achieve the fair presentation of financial statements.

We recommended that the Management, through its Bids and Awards Committee, submit to the audit team copies of awarded contracts together with their supporting documents, duly tabbed for easy reference, within the prescribed period set by the regulations so that any deficiency found could be promptly communicated to the management and immediately corrected to avoid the issuance of suspension during the post-audit.

We recommended and the management agreed to stop the practice of charging expenses to 20% Development Fund which are not related to the nature of investment/capital expenditures and strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of the guidelines set forth under the DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned department heads to:
A. Refrain from using the 20% EDF outside the scope for which the fund was established to achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes that are beneficial to the constituents of the municipality.
B. Adhere strictly to the provisions of the guidelines set forth under the DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular.

We recommended that Municipal Accountant utilize the available manpower and maintain separate books and financial statements of these special accounts in the General Fund so that adequate information could be provided as to its financial status and for sound decision making of the management.

We recommended and the management agreed to:

a) Direct the General Services Officer with the Inventory Committee to conduct
complete physical count and classify properly the accounts presented in the
RPCPPE;

b) Direct the concerned department to complete the one-time cleansing of PPE
accounts intended to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence,
condition, and accountability;

c) Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries in
order to reconcile the balances per financial statements against the RPCPPE
prepared by the General Services Office and make full disclosures of the related
information in the Notes to the Financial Statements;

d) Constantly review and monitor the system-generated depreciation schedule for
the PPE and evaluate the correctness of the data using the underlying provisions and
basis of computation provided in IPSAS 17; and

e) Exert effort to provide a manual computation on the depreciation charges and make
necessary adjusting entries to correct the amount of depreciation of the properties.

We recommended and the management agreed to:

a. Implement the increase in capitalization threshold from P15,000.00 to
P50,000.00 and direct the Municipal Accountant and the General Services
Officer (GSO) to undertake necessary procedures provided under COA
Circular 2022-004;

b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments to
reclassify the properties from PPE accounts to Semi-Expendable Property
accounts; and

c. Direct the Municipal Accountant and the GSO to install measures/controls
over semi-expendable properties such as, but not limited to, update existing
PPE and inventory records, and prepare and maintain necessary
forms/records/reports for purpose of record keeping of these assets.

We recommended and the management agreed to:

a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting journal entries to
correct the previously made entries by including the derecognition of the
corresponding asset;

b) Enjoin the concerned department heads to comply with the applicable laws,
rules and regulations on the disposal of unserviceable properties; and

c) Ensure that the accounting procedures were done in a timely manner and
sound internal control to achieve the fair presentation of financial statements.

We recommended and the management agreed to:

a) Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the Bank
Reconciliation Statements and take up all identified reconciling items;

b) Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to conduct
regular reconciliation of the cash balances so that errors/discrepancies be
adjusted accordingly. Exert maximum effort in tracing and verifying the
accounts; and

c) Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare necessary entries for the
adjustments of affected cash and cash equivalent accounts due to
errors/inaccuracies.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

a. Direct the concerned officials to immediately settle the unliquidated cash
advances. Non-compliance will constitute the valid cause for the withholding
of the salary. Moreover, send demand letters requiring the immediate
liquidation of the cash advances to the concerned employees; and

b. Strictly comply with the applicable rules and regulations pertaining to not
granting any additional cash advances unless the previous ones were
settled first.

We recommended that the Management, through its Bids and Awards Committee,
submit to the audit team the copies of awarded contracts together with their
supporting documents, duly tabbed for easy reference, within the prescribed period
set by the regulations so that any deficiency found could be promptly
communicated to the management and immediately corrected to avoid the issuance
of suspension during the post-audit.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

a) Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the financial reports, DVs and its
supporting documents and the bank reconciliation statements to the Commission
within the deadline set in accordance with the applicable laws, rules and
regulations;

b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare detailed action plan to ensure the
timely submission of the transaction documents to the Office of the Auditor; and

c) Issue the necessary directive to inform and require all departments to adhere
and commit to the action plan and proposed improvements.

We recommended that the Management:

a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to transfer all unexpended/unutilized/
unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust
Fund account;

b) Fully disclose the amount and details of the current year and the previous
years’ unexpended balance of LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements;
and

c) Regularly submit the monthly Reports on Sources and Utilization of LDRRM
Fund to COA and other offices, in accordance with Items 5.1.5 and 5.1.6 of
COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended and the management agreed to stop the practice of charging
expenses to 20% Development Fund which are not related to and do not partake
the nature of investment/ capital expenditures and strictly adhere to the pertinent
provisions of DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1.

observations.managementAction: AAR No 2022 inventory team was created.
observations.reason: The management is not equipped to conduct one-time cleansing.

observations.recommendation: In view of the foregoing observation, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit Bank Reconciliation Statements and take up all identified book reconciling items to ascertain the accuracy of the reported balances of the cash accounts.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2022

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned department heads to:
a. Refrain from using the 20% EDF outside the scope for which the fund was established to achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes that are beneficial to the constituents of the municipality; and
b. Submit to the audit team the lacking supporting documents such as the official receipt for the payment of floodlight lamps and the list of the medical and other LGU personnel directly involved in the vaccination program; and
c. Adhere strictly to the provisions of the guidelines set forth under the DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular.
observations.managementAction: And management agreed to direct the concerned department heads to: A. Refrain from using the 20% EDF outside the scope for which the fund was established to achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes that are beneficial to the constituents of the municipality. B. Submit to the audit team the lacking supporting documents such as the official receipt for the payment of floodlight lamps and the list of the medical and other LGU personnel directly involved in the vaccination program. C. Adhere strictly to the provisions of the guidelines set forth under the DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Direct the concerned officials to immediately settle the unliquidated cash advances. Non-compliance will constitute the valid cause for the withholding of the salary. Moreover, send demand letters requiring the immediate liquidation of the cash advances to the concerned employees; and
b. Strictly comply with the applicable rules and regulations pertaining to not granting any additional cash advances unless the previous ones were settled first.
observations.managementAction: And management agreed to direct the concerned officials to immediately settle the unliquidated cash advances. Non-compliance will constitute the valid cause for the withholding of the salary. Moreover, send demand letters requiring the immediate liquidation of the cash advances to the concerned employees; and Strictly comply with the applicable rules and regulations pertaining to not granting any additional cash advances unless the previous ones were settled first.
observations.reason: Some accountable officers issued promissory notes after receiving their respective demand letters.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Accountant the following:
a. Prepare and certify correct the monthly Reports on Sources and Utilization of LDRRM Fund (LDRRMF) and regularly submit to COA and other offices in accordance with Sections 5.1.5 and 5.1.6 of COA Circular No. 2012-002; 
b. Facilitate the immediate reconciliation of accounts and transfer all unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund account; and
c. Fully disclose the amount and details of the current year and the previous years’ unexpended balance of LDRRM Fund in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: And management agreed to: a. Facilitate the immediate reconciliation of accounts and transfer all unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund account; b. Fully disclose the amount and details of the current year and the previous years’ unexpended balance of LDRRM Fund in the Notes to Financial Statements; and c. Regularly submit the monthly Reports on Sources and Utilization of LDRRM Fund to COA and other offices, in accordance with Items 5.1.5 and 5.1.6 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Require the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to: i. Reconcile the RPCPPE with the Accounting Records to establish the correct details of PPE account; ii. Maintain proper and complete record of the properties as stipulated under the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume 1; b. Require the General Services Officer to conduct completely the physical inventory count of Property, Plant and Equipment; and Instruct the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments for the recognition of depreciation of PPEs in SEF and TF books.
observations.managementAction: And the Management agreed to: a) Require the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to: i. Reconcile the RPCPPE with the Accounting Records to establish the correct details of PPE account; ii. Maintain proper and complete record of the properties as stipulated under the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume 1; b) Require the General Services Officer to conduct completely the physical inventory count of Property, Plant and Equipment; and Instruct the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments for the recognition of depreciation of PPEs in SEF and TF books.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant:
a. Strictly monitor the status of cash advances and send demand letters requiring the immediate liquidation of the cash advances; 
b. Strictly comply with the applicable rules and regulations pertaining to not granting any additional cash advances unless the previous ones were settled first; and
c. Make an accounting journal entry to record the unliquidated amount of P1,000,000.00 in the Cash Advances to Special Disbursing Officer account.
observations.managementAction: And the management agreed to: a. Strictly monitor the status of cash advances and send demand letters requiring the immediate liquidation of the cash advances; b. Strictly comply with the applicable rules and regulations pertaining to not granting any additional cash advances unless the previous ones were settled first; and c. Make an accounting journal entry to record the unliquidated amount of P1,000,000.00 in the Cash Advances to Special Disbursing Officer account.
observations.reason: Some accountable officers issued promissory notes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to: Direct the LDRRM Officer to: a) Prepare a thorough Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) which include both the current appropriations and unexpended prior years balances; and b) Regularly submit the monthly Reports on Sources and Utilization of LDRRM Fund to COA and other offices, in accordance with Sections 5.1.5 and 5.1.6 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: And management agreed to direct the LDRRM Officer to prepare a thorough Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) which include both the current appropriations and unexpended prior years balances; and regularly submit the monthly Reports on Sources and Utilization of LDRRM Fund to COA and other offices, in accordance with Sections 5.1.5 and 5.1.6 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.reason: Personnel were not aware of the monthly submission of reports to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the management agreed to: a) Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the annual financial statements in a prescribed format within the deadline set by the Commission; b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare detailed action plan to ensure the timely submission of the vouchers to the Office of the Auditor. Require the Municipal Accountant to submit also the remaining disbursement vouchers together with supporting documents; and c) Require the Municipal Accountant to strategize to get a copy of the bank statement as of each prescribed period by requesting from its depository banks. Moreover, ensure that bank reconciliation is done and submitted within the prescribed period as stated in the above-cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: And the management agreed to: a) Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the annual financial statements in a prescribed format within the deadline set by the Commission; b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare detailed action plan to ensure the timely submission of the vouchers to the Office of the Auditor. Require the Municipal Accountant to submit also the remaining disbursement vouchers together with supporting documents; and c) Require the Municipal Accountant to strategize to get a copy of the bank statement as of each prescribed period by requesting from its depository banks. Moreover, ensure that bank reconciliation is done and submitted within the prescribed period as stated in the above-cited regulations.
observations.reason: Lacking accounting personnel with technical knowledge that could assist the accountant in the preparation of FS and bank reconciliation statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant and all others concerned to make the submission of the above-mentioned documents possible. Strictly comply with the prescribed deadlines of prior reports submission and cause the submission of the remaining unsubmitted reports to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: And management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant and all others concerned to make the submission of the above-mentioned documents possible. Strictly comply with the prescribed deadlines of prior reports submission and cause the submission of the remaining unsubmitted reports to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason: Submission of DVs was delayed due to lacking supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to maintain separate books and separate financial statements of these special accounts in the General Fund so that adequate information could be provided as to its financial status and sound decision making of the management.
observations.managementAction: And management agreed to maintain separate books and separate financial statements of these special accounts in the General Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Accountant and other responsible officials comply with required submissions and ensure timely provision of supporting documents to avoid delays and allow proper audit evaluation.
observations.managementAction: And management agreed to comply and ensure timely submissions.
observations.reason: Lack of staff and delays in compiling voluminous supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management:
a) Municipal Treasurer confer with the source agency/ies for the proper disposition of unutilized balance after completion of project, if not specified in the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA). Prepare a refund to source agency/ies with completed projects and dormant bank deposits, if warranted. Also, update the status of other dormant bank accounts to avoid bank charges;
b) The Municipal Accountant exert extra efforts to prepare and submit timely the bank reconciliation statements and take up all book reconciling items to ascertain the accuracy of the reported balances of the cash accounts; and
c) The Sangguniang Bayan execute a resolution for the closing of the accounts being maintained in the Philippine National Bank and transfer the balance to the General Fund Account in Development Bank of the Philippines, Kidapawan City Branch to be available for appropriation.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 Management was not process of reconciling the balance. to lack of accounting staff with technical skills who can be assigned or assist the Accountant in the preparation of the BRS.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management that the Municipal Accountant make reclassification entries to appropriately account the affected accounts in the financial statements and subsequently record all the transactions as prescribed in the Government Accounting Manual for Local Government Units.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed on the following:
a) The Municipal Budget Officer ensure that the augmentations or realignments have the legal authority and procedures and guidelines are strictly followed before an obligation request is approved;
b) The Local Finance Committee (LFC), in coordination with the local officials concerned, to prepare a monthly utilization report in the format provided in DBM LBC 125 annex C and regularly post it in at least three conspicuous public spaces and LGU’s website to keep the community informed and updated on the use of public funds; and
c) The Municipal Accountant prepare an adjusting entry to recognize the liability on the unexpended portion of the fund.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed on the following:
a) The LDRRM Council, through the LDRRM officer, prepare a complete and comprehensive LDRRMFIP which should include both the current appropriations and the unexpended previous years’ balances;
b) The Municipal Accountant:
i. Immediately transfer all the unexpended/unobligated balances of the current and previous year’s QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund; ii. Record all procurement and issuance of supplies to the appropriate asset and expense accounts which are charged against LDRRM Fund. Maintain ledger cards to monitor the movement of balances; iii. Fully disclose the amount and details of the current year and the previous years’ unexpended balance of LDRRM Fund in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 agreed on the following:
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed, with the Municipal Accountant as lead, to submit the list of recipients indicating therein the specific relief goods distributed and received with dates of distribution/receipt, among other information to make possible and proper accounting for the relief operations procured and conducted. It may also help validate whether the activities benefited the targeted beneficiaries as planned.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed on the following:
a) The Municipal Accounting Office will exert extra efforts to prepare and submit timely the bank reconciliation with the books to ascertain the accuracy of the reported balances of the cash accounts; and
b) The Human Resource Officer, as lead, will consider hiring additional personnel to enable the accounting office cope with its functions efficiently.
observations.managementAction: Monthly BRS was not submitted to the Office of the Auditor for verification due to lack of accounting staff with technical skills who can be assigned or assist the Accountant in the preparation of the BRS.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant:
a) Issue the final notice of demand to the accountable officers requiring full liquidation of outstanding cash advances; and apply stern measures such as withholding the salaries and other emoluments of officials/employees who continuously disregard, fail or decline to comply with the demand and stop the granting of additional cash advance; b) Ensure strict compliance with COA Circulars 97-002 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, particularly the period within which to liquidate the same to avoid accumulation of unsettled cash advances; c) Make an accounting journal entry to record the unliquidated amount of P1,000,000.00 in the Cash Advances to Special Disbursing Officer account; d) The Municipal Treasurer will ascertain the presence of the “accountant’s certification that the previous cash advance has been liquidated” before processing another cash advance of the accountable officer concerned; e) The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer will ensure that elected officials are not granted with cash advances other than those expressly granted by the existing laws, rules and regulations and only permanently appointed officials and employees are designated as disbursing officers; and f) The LGU officials and employees should strictly adhere to the regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances within the prescribed period.

We recommended and the Management agreed on the following:
a) The LDRRM Council, through the LDRRM officer, must prepare a complete and comprehensive LDRRMFIP which should include both the current appropriations and the unexpended previous year balances;
b) The Municipal Accountant should: i. Record all procurement and issuance of supplies to the appropriate asset and expense accounts which are charged against LDRRM Fund. Maintain ledger cards to monitor the movement of balances of goods and supplies procured; ii. Facilitate the immediate reconciliation of accounts and transfer all unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund account; iii. Make the reclassification entry to reflect the accurate balance of Trust Liabilities-DRRMF and Due to LGUs account in the books; iv. Fully disclose the amount and details of the current year and the previous years’ unexpended balance of LDRRM Fund in the Notes to Financial Statements; and
c) The Inventory Committee, through the Municipal General Services Officer, must conduct the actual physical count of inventories. Likewise, the Municipal General Services Officer shall maintain stock cards and submit the reconciled inventory report to the resident auditor.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 agreed on the following: a) The LDRRM Council, through the LDRRM officer, must prepare a complete and comprehensive LDRRMFIP which should include both the current appropriations and the unexpended previous year balances; b) The Municipal Accountant: i. Record all procurement and issuance of supplies to the appropriate asset and expense accounts which are charged against LDRRM Fund. Maintain ledger cards to monitor the movement of balances; ii. Facilitate the immediate reconciliation of accounts and transfer all unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund account; iii. Make the reclassification entry to reflect the accurate balance of Trust Liabilities-DRRMF and Due to LGUs account in the books; iv. Fully disclose the amount and details of the current year and the previous years’ unexpended balance of LDRRM Fund in the Notes to Financial Statements; and c) The Inventory Committee, through the Municipal General Services Officer, must conduct the actual physical count of inventories.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management create an inventory committee to conduct the complete physical count of all PPE. A Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) as of year-end should be accomplished, duly reconciled with the accounting records and submitted to the COA not later than January 31 of the ensuing year. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant and MGSO should maintain complete PPE ledger cards and property cards, respectively, for each property for easy reference and reconciliation of assets.
observations.managementAction: 2017 & 2018 AAR: RPCPPE should be accomplished, duly reconciled and submitted to COA. MGSO and Municipal Accountant to maintain complete PPE ledger cards and property cards, respectively.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the GSO to gather the relevant documents and complete details of the subject PPE items to take up the necessary adjustments on depreciation and accumulated surplus. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant and MGSO should maintain complete PPE ledger cards and property cards, respectively, for each property for easy reference and reconciliation of assets.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 & 2018 advised Municipal Accountant and GSO to gather relevant documents and take up necessary adjustments on depreciation.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant and all others concerned to make the submission of the above-mentioned possible. Strict adherence to prescribed deadlines of reports submission and cause the submission of the remaining reports to the Office of the Auditor. Strictly comply with prescribed documentation to ensure validity of transactions.
observations.managementAction: 2018 AAR informed management to submit the remaining reports and documents; strict compliance with deadlines required.
observations.reason: Submission of DVs was delayed due to lacking supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly exhaust all remedies for the full settlement or liquidation of cash advances including issuance of demand letters, withholding of salaries of officials and employees who fail/refuse to settle, apply stern measures and ensure strict compliance with COA Circulars 97-002. Make accounting entries to properly record advances and ensure liquidation.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 & 2018 urged Municipal Accountant to issue final notice/demand and apply measures; Municipal Treasurer to ensure accountant’s certification before granting new advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management adhere to the principle of transparency, accountability, efficiency and economy in the disbursement of government funds by ensuring that all disbursement vouchers are properly signed by authorized officials and necessary supporting documents are attached for the claims/transactions; otherwise these will be suspended or disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: 2018 AAR: Management to ensure all DVs are properly supported; otherwise transactions will be suspended or disallowed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management utilize the available manpower and finance committee to maintain separate books and financial statements of the special accounts in the General Fund so that adequate information could be provided as to its financial status and for sound decision making of the management.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 advised Municipal Accountant to maintain separate books and financial statements for economic enterprises; Municipal Planning & Development Council Officer and Municipal Engineer to closely monitor the implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to create a Project & Monitoring Team (PMT) to undertake regular monitoring of project implementation to ensure timely and proper implementation of all prioritized programs and projects under the 20% EDF so that the desired socio-economic development outcomes could be achieved. The Municipal Planning and Development Council Officer and Municipal Engineer should closely monitor the implementation of the proposed programs and projects. The monitoring procedure should include periodic reporting of the progress or delays in the implementation to assist the management in identifying gaps and allow prompt corrective action.
observations.managementAction: 2017 AAR: Management to form PMT and ensure monitoring and reporting.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Cash Management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and update the reconciliation of the Cash in Bank accounts and give particular attention to the reconciling items. Distribute copies of the BRS to the Audit Team and the bank through its accounting staff to be aware of their errors and take appropriate action. Make proper adjustments in the books of accounts of the reconciling items for fair presentation of the Cash in Bank account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 & 2020: Accounting staff to update reconciliation and distribute copies of BRS to Audit Team and bank. Employ accounting staff this 2016 accounting staff this part was informed.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed on the following: a. The Municipal Accounting Office will exert extra efforts to prepare and submit timely the bank reconciliation statements to ascertain the accuracy of the reported balances of the cash accounts. b. The Human Resource Officer, as lead, will consider hiring additional personnel to enable the accounting office cope with its functions efficiently.
observations.managementAction: Hire additional employee regularly submitted to the Office of the Auditor for verification due to lack of accounting staff with technical skills who can be assigned or assist the Accountant in the preparation of the BRS.
observations.reason: Monthly BRS was not prepared and submitted to the Office of the Auditor for verification due to lack of accounting staff with technical skills who can be assigned or assist the Accountant in the preparation of the BRS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Accountant utilize the available manpower and maintain separate books and financial statements of these special accounts in the General Fund so that adequate information could be provided as to its financial status and for sound decision making of the management.
observations.managementAction: Complied audit recommendations. Implement separate books and statements.
observations.reason: Special accounts and financial statements were not prepared and maintained due to lack of personnel.

observations.recommendation: Management should require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and update the reconciliation of the Cash in Bank accounts and give particular attention to the reconciling items. Distribute copies of the BRS to the Audit Team and the bank thru its Audit Team Leader for them to be aware of their errors and take the appropriate action of the reconciling items. Make proper adjustments in the books of accounts for the reconciling items for fair presentation of the Cash in Bank account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Employ accounting staff this 2020 submitted to the Office of the Auditor for verification due to lack of accounting staff with technical skills who can be assigned or assist the Accountant in the preparation of the BRS.
observations.reason: Monthly BRS was not prepared and submitted to the Office of the Auditor for verification due to lack of accounting staff with technical skills who can be assigned or assist the Accountant in the preparation of the BRS.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed on the following:
a) The Municipal Treasurer confer with the source agency/ies for the proper
disposition of unutilized balance after completion of project, if not specified in the
Memorandum of Agreement (MOA). Prepare a refund to source agency/ies with
completed projects and dormant bank deposits, if warranted. Also, update the
status of other dormant bank accounts to avoid bank charges.
b) The Municipal Accountant exert extra efforts to prepare and submit timely the
bank reconciliation statements and take up all book reconciling items to ascertain
the accuracy of the reported balances of the cash accounts.
c) The Sangguniang Bayan execute a resolution for the closing of the accounts being
maintained in the Philippine National Bank and transfer the balance to the
General Fund Account in Development Bank of the Philippines, Kidapawan City
Branch to be available for appropriation.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant make
reclassification entries to appropriately account the affected accounts in the financial
statements and subsequently record all the transactions as prescribed in the
Government Accounting Manual for Local Government Units.

We recommended and the Management agreed on the following:
a) The Municipal Budget Officer ensure that the augmentations or realignments
have the legal authority and procedures and guidelines are strictly followed
before an obligation request is approved;
b) The Local Finance Committee (LFC), in coordination with the other local
officials concerned, to prepare a monthly utilization report in the format
provided in DBM LBC 125 annex C and regularly post it in at least three
conspicuous public spaces and LGU’s website to keep the community informed
and updated on the use of public funds; and
c) The Municipal Accountant prepare an adjusting entry to recognize the liability
on the unexpended portion of the fund.

We recommended and the Management agreed on the following:
a) The LDRRM Council, through the LDRRM officer, prepare a complete and
comprehensive LDRRMFIP which should include both the current
appropriations and the unexpended previous years’ balances;
b) The Municipal Accountant:
i. Immediately transfer all the unexpended/unobligated balances of the
current and previous year’s QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust
Fund;
ii. Record all procurement and issuance of supplies to the appropriate asset and
expense accounts which are charged against LDRRM Fund. Maintain ledger
cards to monitor the movement of balances;
iii. Fully disclose the amount and details of the current year and the previous
years’ unexpended balance of LDRRM Fund in the Notes to Financial
Statements;

We recommended and the Management agreed, with the Municipal Accountant as
lead, to submit the list of recipients indicating therein the specific relief goods
distributed and received with dates of distribution/receipt, among other information to
make possible and proper accounting for the relief operations procured and
conducted. It may also help validate whether the activities benefited the targeted
beneficiaries as planned.

We recommended and the Management agreed on the following: a. The Municipal Accountant should: i. Issue the final notice of demand to the accountable officers. Withholding of salary/ies of officials and employees who still fail or refuse to settle and/or liquidate their cash advances after the issuance of the final demand may be instituted in accordance with the regulation; ii. Ascertain the transactions pertaining to unliquidated cash advances of ₱11,014,634.32 and require its immediate reconciliation and liquidation thereof; iii. Ensure recording of the cash advances for Confidential Fund in the Advances to Special Disbursing account to properly establish the accountability of the accountable officer; iv. Make an accounting journal entry to record the unliquidated cash advances for Confidential Fund amounting to ₱4,250,000.00 in Advances to Special Disbursing Officer account. This may be subsequently liquidated upon presentment of documents to resident auditor; b. The Municipal Treasurer will ascertain the presence of the “accountant’s certification that the previous cash advance has been liquidated” before processing another cash advance of the accountable officer concerned; c. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer will ensure that elected officials are not granted with cash advances other than those expressly granted by the existing laws, rules and regulations and only permanently appointed officials and employees are designated as disbursing officers; and d. The LGU officials and employees should strictly adhere to the regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: Informed concern employee & officials. No cash advance leniency in the courses of 2017 allowed for enforcement on the rules and regulations and unavailability of supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed on the following: a. The LDRRM Council, through the LDRRM officer, must prepare a complete and comprehensive LDRRMFIP which should include both the current appropriations and the unexpended prior year balances; b. The Municipal Accountant should: i. Record all procurement and issuance of supplies to the appropriate asset and expense accounts which are charged against LDRRM Fund. Maintain ledger cards to monitor the movement of balances of goods and supplies procured; ii. Facilitate the immediate reconciliation of accounts and transfer all unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund account; iii. Make the reclassification entry to reflect the accurate balance of Trust Liabilities-DRRMF and Due to LGUs account in the books; iv. Fully disclose the amount and details of the current year and the previous years’ unexpended balance of LDRRM Fund in the Notes to Financial Statements; and c. The Inventory Committee, through the Municipal General Services Officer, must conduct the actual physical count of inventories. Likewise, the Municipal General Services Officer shall maintain stock cards and submit the reconciled inventory report to the resident auditor.
observations.managementAction: Implement the proposed project as planned and the Project Monitoring Committee to conduct visitation to all completed and on-going programs and projects. The Municipal Accountant, GSO and MDRRMO agreed to have the inventory report prepared for the purchased food supplies and Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE). Also, they will require the Requisition and Issue Slip from end user for any request of food supplies and PPEs for proper recording of items.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed on the following: a. The Agency Authorize Officer should ensure that net take home pay of an employee is sufficient to cover the regular monthly amortization before approving their loan applications; b. The Payroll-in-Charge should prioritize the deduction from the salary of employees the obligations due to GSIS over and above other obligations in the preparation and approval of payrolls; c. The Municipal Accountant should verify if actual deductions are in agreement with the monthly GSIS billings; and d. The Management should assign personnel to undertake reconciliation of the GSIS deductions and remittances against the records of the GSIS to ensure correctness thereof and to establish appropriate measure/s to be undertaken so as to correct errors and/or discrepancies incurred.
observations.managementAction: Employ accounting staff this 2020 submitted to the Office of the Auditor for verification due to lack of accounting staff with technical skills who can be assigned or assist the Accountant in the preparation of the BRS.
observations.reason: Deduct amortization from employees payroll and remit the same to GSIS

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management create an inventory committee to conduct the complete physical count of all PPE. A Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) should be accomplished, duly reconciled with the accounting records and submitted to the COA not later than January 31 of the ensuing year. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant and MGSO should maintain complete PPE ledger cards and property cards, respectively, for each property for easy reference and reconciliation of assets.
observations.managementAction: Complied audit recommendations. Take up necessary adjustments on every account. Adjustments should be made as soon as actual physical count and reconciliation of inventories are completed. Corresponding copies of the JEV and inventory reports should be furnished to the audit team leader soonest.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of accounts was not made due to time constraints. Adjustments should be made as soon as actual count and reconciliation are completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the GSO to gather the relevant documents and complete details of the subject PPE items and take up the necessary adjustments on depreciation and accumulated surplus. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant and MGSO should maintain complete PPE ledger cards and property cards, respectively, for each property for easy reference and reconciliation of assets.
observations.managementAction: Take up necessary adjusting entries & depreciation due to incomplete details of the account.
observations.reason: Some PPE accounts were not provided with adjustments & depreciation due to incomplete details of the account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant and all others concerned to make the submission of the above-mentioned documents possible. Strictly comply with the prescribed deadlines of submission and cause the submission of the remaining unsubmitted reports to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: Submit unsubmitted accounts including paid DVs covering the period September to December 2018. Strict adherence to the prescribed rules and regulation on the disbursement processes should be observed so as to ensure complete documentation of all government transactions prior to payment thereof.
observations.reason: Submission of DVs was delayed due to lacking supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management exhaust all remedies for the full settlement or liquidation of outstanding cash advances & including enforcement of hereunder courses of action: 1. Demand full liquidation of outstanding unliquidated cash advances from concerned officers and employees; 2. Apply stern measures such as withholding of the salaries and other emoluments of the officials/employees who continuously disregard, fail or decline to comply with the demand and stop the granting of additional cash advance; 3. Ensure strict compliance with COA Circulars 97-002 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, particularly the period within which to liquidate the same to avoid accumulation of unsettled cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Advised treasury department not to release checks w/ incomplete unsigned documents. It is emphasized that the proper documents should be properly observed by every disbursement office. Inform every department through the Budget, Accounting and the Treasury in the announcement during the monthly convocation and program.
observations.reason: Cash advances remained unliquidated due to leniency in the application of rules and unavailability of supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management should adhere to the principle of transparency, and accountability, efficiency and economy in disbursement of government funds by ensuring that all disbursement vouchers are properly signed by authorized officials and necessary documents are completely attached as support for the aforecited department through the Budget, Accounting and the Treasury in the processing of the DV and prior to payment thereof; otherwise these will be suspended or disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Advised concerned department and informed every personnel to comply with supporting documents before checking released of checks.
observations.reason: Some disbursement vouchers lack supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to create a Project Monitoring Team (PMT) to undertake regular monitoring of project implementation to ensure timely and proper implementation of all the prioritized programs and projects under the 20% EDF so that the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes could redound to its intended beneficiaries at the soonest time. The Municipal Planning and Development Council Officer and Municipal Engineer should closely monitor the implementation of the proposed programs and projects. The monitoring procedure should include periodic reporting of the progress or delays in the implementation to assist the management in the performance review and in identifying gaps in implementation to allow prompt corrective action.
observations.managementAction: Implement the proposed project as planned even beyond 2017 the target implementation date. The Project Monitoring Committee to conduct visitation to all completed and on-going programs and projects.
observations.reason: Full implementation was affected by the pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Accountant utilize the available manpower and maintain separate books and financial statements of these special accounts in the General Fund so that adequate information could be provided as to its financial status and for sound decision making of the management. The LCE is enjoined to give focus to this considering that the Accounting processes and procedures are still being done manually, thus adequate number of personnel are indeed necessary to ensure timely and correct recording and reporting of government transactions.
observations.managementAction: Complied audit recommendations
observations.reason: Special accounts and financial statements were not prepared and maintained due to lack of personnel.

observations.recommendation: Management should require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and update the reconciliation of the Cash in Bank accounts and give particular attention to the reconciling items. Distribute copies of the BRS to the Audit Team and the bank thru its Audit Team Leader for them to be aware of their errors and discrepancies noted. Make proper adjustments in the books of accounts of the reconciling items for fair presentation of the Cash in Bank account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Employ accounting staff this 2020
observations.reason: Monthly BRS was not regularly prepared and submitted to the Office of the Auditor for verification due to lack of accounting staff with technical skills who can be assigned or assist the Accountant in the preparation of the BRS.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed on the following:
a. The Municipal Accounting Office will exert extra efforts to prepare and
submit timely the bank reconciliation statements to ascertain the accuracy
of the reported balances of the cash accounts.
b. The Human Resource Officer, as lead, will consider hiring additional
personnel to enable the accounting office cope with its functions
efficiently.

We recommended and the Management agreed on the following:
a. The Municipal Accountant should:
i. Issue the final notice of demand to the accountable officers.
Withholding of salary/ies of officials and employees who still fail or
refuse to settle and/or liquidate their cash advances after the issuance
of the final demand may be instituted in accordance with the
regulation;
ii. Ascertain the transactions pertaining to unliquidated cash advances of
₱11,014,634.32 and require its immediate reconciliation and
liquidation thereof;
iii. Ensure recording of the cash advances for Confidential Fund in the
Advances to Special Disbursing account to properly establish the
accountability of the accountable officer;
iv. Make an accounting journal entry to record the unliquidated cash
advances for Confidential Fund amounting to ₱4,250,000,00 in
Advances to Special Disbursing Officer account. This may be
subsequently liquidated upon presentment of documents to resident
auditor;
b. The Municipal Treasurer will ascertain the presence of the “accountant’s
certification that the previous cash advance has been liquidated” before
processing another cash advance of the accountable officer concerned;
c. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer will ensure that
elected officials are not granted with cash advances other than those
expressly granted by the existing laws, rules and regulations and only
permanently appointed officials and employees are designated as
disbursing officers; and
d. The LGU officials and employees should strictly adhere to the regulations
on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances within the
prescribed period.

We recommended and the Management agreed on the following:
a. The LDRRM Council, through the LDRRM officer, must prepare a
complete and comprehensive LDRRMFIP which should include both the
current appropriations and the unexpended prior year balances;
b. The Municipal Accountant should:
i. Record all procurement and issuance of supplies to the appropriate
asset and expense accounts which are charged against LDRRM
Fund. Maintain ledger cards to monitor the movement of balances
of goods and supplies procured;
ii. Facilitate the immediate reconciliation of accounts and transfer all
unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE to
the Special Trust Fund account;
iii. Make the reclassification entry to reflect the accurate balance of
Trust Liabilities-DRRMF and Due to LGUs account in the books;
iv. Fully disclose the amount and details of the current year and the
previous years’ unexpended balance of LDRRM Fund in the Notes
to Financial Statements; and
c. The Inventory Committee, through the Municipal General Services
Officer, must conduct the actual physical count of inventories. Likewise,
the Municipal General Services Officer shall maintain stock cards and
submit the reconciled inventory report to the resident auditor.

We recommended and Management agreed on the following:
a. The Agency Authorize Officer should ensure that net take home pay of an
employee is sufficient to cover the regular monthly amortization before
approving their loan applications;
b. The Payroll-in-Charge should prioritize the deduction from the salary of
employees the obligations due to GSIS over and above other obligations in
the preparation and approval of payrolls;
c. The Municipal Accountant should verify if actual deductions are in
agreement with the monthly GSIS billings. and
d. The Management should assign personnel to undertake reconciliation of the
GSIS deductions and remittances against the records of the GSIS to ensure
correctness thereof and to establish appropriate measure/s to be
undertaken so as to correct errors and/or discrepancies incurred.

We recommended that management create an inventory committee to conduct the complete Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE). A Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) should be accomplished, duly reconciled with the accounting records and submitted to the COA not later than January 31 of the ensuing year. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant and MGSO should maintain complete PPE ledger cards and property cards, respectively, for each property for easy reference and reconciliation of assets. Corresponding copies of the JEV and inventory reports should be furnished to the audit team leader soonest.
observations.managementAction: Accountant confer w/ GSO for an inventory & update & implement necessary adjustments on every account.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of accounts was not made due to time constraints. Adjustments should be made as soon as actual physical count and reconciliation of inventories are completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the GSO to gather the relevant documents and complete & take up necessary adjustments on depreciation and accumulated surplus. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant and MGSO should maintain complete PPE ledger cards and property cards, respectively, for each property for easy reference and reconciliation of assets. Pease furnish the audit team of the JEV recognizing the same in its books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Take up necessary adjusting entries & depreciation
observations.reason: Some PPE accounts were not provided with complete details of the account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant and all others concerned to make the submission of the above-mentioned documents possible. Strictly comply with the prescribed deadlines of submission and cause the submission of the remaining unsubmitted reports to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: Submit unsubmitted accounts including paid DVs covering the period September to December 2018
observations.reason: Submission of DVs was delayed due to lacking supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management exhaust all remedies for the full settlement or liquidation of outstanding cash advances & including enforcement of hereunder courses of action: 1. Demand full liquidation of outstanding cash advances from concerned officers and employees; 2. Apply stern measures such as withholding of the salaries and other emoluments of the officials/employees who continuously disregard, fail or decline to comply with the demand and stop the granting of additional cash advance; 3. Ensure strict compliance with COA Circulars 97-002 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, particularly the period within which to liquidate the same to avoid accumulation of unsettled cash advances. Other allowable actions may be adopted by the management to ensure liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Informed concern employee & officials. No cash advance unliquidated.
observations.reason: Cash advances remained unliquidated due to leniency in the enforcement on the rules and unavailability of supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management should adhere to the principle of transparency, and accountability, efficiency and economy in disbursement ensuring that all disbursement vouchers are properly signed by authorized officials and necessary documents are completely attached for the aforecited claims/transactions, otherwise these will be suspended or disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Advised treasury department not to release checks w/ incomplete unsigned documents. Said practice of the management should already be stopped. Inform every concerned department through announcement during monthly convocation program.
observations.reason: Some disbursement vouchers lack supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: 1. To ensure strict utilization of LDRRM funds for calamity/disaster-related programs/activities in order to align with the funds’ purpose and objectives. 2. To revisit, review, and evaluate the programs/projects/activities prepared in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) of the Municipality to ensure effective formulation implementation and utilization of LDRRM Fund.
observations.managementAction: Complied with audit recommendations

observations.recommendation: 1. Ensure that projects identified by the barangays should be properly and carefully planned and studied by conducting a survey on the development issues of the barangays. 2. Freeze and recover the funds transferred from those recipient barangays that failed to adhere to the provisions stated in 2.3. and not to release succeeding funds in the absence of required documents. 3. Include in the MOA a provision on the liquidation of the funds transferred in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: Proper review/ monitoring of respective barangay submitted by respective barangay

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to create a Project Monitoring Team (PMT) to undertake regular monitoring of project & implementation to ensure timely and proper implementation of all the prioritized programs and projects under the 20% EDF so that the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes could redound to its intended beneficiaries at the soonest time. Also, the Municipal Planning and Development Council Officer and Municipal Engineer should closely monitor the implementation of the proposed programs and projects. The monitoring procedure should include periodic reporting of the progress or delays in the implementation to assist the management in the performance review and in identifying gaps in implementation to allow prompt corrective action.
observations.managementAction: Implement the proposed project as planned even beyond 2017
observations.reason: Implementation was delayed due to weather conditions, insufficient personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the inventory committee to conduct complete physical count of all PPE and immediately reconcile the report with the accounting records to ensure fair presentation the balances of PPE accounts in the financial statements. We also recommended to prepare the RPCPPE by type to easily facilitate the reconciliation with the accounting records. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant and MGSO should maintain complete PPE ledger cards and property cards, respectively.
observations.managementAction: AAR No action taken CY 2017
observations.reason: Inventory Committee was not created to facilitate the conduct of actual inventory taking of all agency’s assets. (Reiteration of CY 2016 observation)

observations.recommendation: Management should require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and update the reconciliation of the Cash in Bank accounts and give particular attention to the reconciling items. Distribute copies of the BRS to the Audit Team and the bank thru its Audit Team Leader for them to be aware of their errors and take the appropriate action the discrepancies noted. Make proper adjustments in the books of accounts of the reconciling items for fair presentation of the Cash in Bank account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR The Municipal Accountant was not regularly prepared and updated the reconciliation of the cash in bank accounts
observations.reason: Monthly BRS was prepared by the Accountant for the GF accounts only and committed to submit same to COA auditor. Lack of accounting staff with technical skills who can be assigned or assist the Accountant in the preparation of the BRS.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management create an inventory committee to conduct the complete
physical count of all PPE. A Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment
(RPCPPE) should be accomplished, duly reconciled with the accounting records and submitted to
the COA not later than January 31 of the ensuing year.

Likewise, the Municipal Accountant and MGSO should maintain complete PPE ledger
cards and property cards, respectively, for each property for easy reference and reconciliation of
assets.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended to the Municipal Accountant in coordination
with the GSO to gather the relevant documents and complete details of the subject PPE items and
take up the necessary adjustments on depreciation and accumulated surplus.

Likewise, the Municipal Accountant and MGSO should maintain complete PPE ledger
cards and property cards, respectively, for each property for easy reference and reconciliation of
assets.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant and all others
concerned to make the submission of the above-mentioned documents possible. Strictly comply
with the prescribed deadlines of reports submission and cause the submission of the remaining
unsubmitted reports to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that management strictly exhaust all remedies for the full settlement or
liquidation of outstanding cash advances including enforcement of hereunder courses of action:
1. Demand full liquidation of outstanding cash advances from concerned officers and
employees;
2. Apply stern measures such as withholding the salaries and other emoluments of the
officials/employees who continuously disregard, fail or decline to comply with the
demand and stop the granting of additional cash advance;
3. Ensure strict compliance with COA Circulars 97-002 on the grant, utilization and
liquidation of cash advances, particularly the period within which to liquidate the same
to avoid accumulation of unsettled cash advances.

We recommend that Management should adhere to the principle of transparency,
accountability, efficiency and economy in the disbursement of government funds by ensuring that
all disbursement vouchers are properly signed by authorized officials and the necessary documents
are completely attached in support for the aforecited claims/transactions, otherwise these will be
suspended or disallowed in audit.

1. To ensure strict utilization of LDRRM funds for calamity/disaster-related
programs/activities in order to align with the funds’ purpose and objectives.
2. To revisit, review, and evaluate the programs/projects/activities prepared in the Local
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) of the
Municipality to ensure effective formulation implementation and utilization of LDRRM
Fund.

We recommended that Municipal Accountant utilize the available manpower and maintain
separate books and financial statements of these special accounts in the General Fund so that
adequate information could be provided as to its financial status and for sound decision making
of the management.

1. Ensure that projects identified by the barangays should be properly and carefully planned
and studied by conducting a survey on the development issues of the barangays.
2. Freeze and recover the funds transferred from those recipient barangays that failed to
adhere to the provisions stated in 2.3.
3. Include in the MOA a provision on the liquidation of the funds transferred in accordance
with COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended to the management to create a Project Monitoring Team (PMT) to
undertake regular monitoring of project implementation to ensure timely and proper
implementation of all the prioritized programs and projects under the 20% EDF so that the desired
socio-economic development and environmental outcomes could redound to its intended
beneficiaries at the soonest time possible.

Also, the Municipal Planning and Development Council Officer and Municipal Engineer
should closely monitor the implementation of the proposed programs and projects. The monitoring
procedure should include periodic reporting of the progress or delays in the implementation to
assist the management in the performance review and in identifying gaps in implementation to
allow prompt corrective action.

We recommended to the Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory of all PPE and reconcile the results of the count with the accounting records. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the GSO should gather the relevant documents and complete details of the subject PPE items and take up the necessary adjustments on depreciation and accumulated surplus. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant and MGSO should maintain complete PPE ledger cards and property cards, respectively, for each property for easy reference and reconciliation of assets.
observations.managementAction: AAR Take up necessary adjusting entries CY 2017
observations.reason: Relevant documents and complete details of subject PPE entries were not yet obtained

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management demand full liquidation of outstanding cash advances from concerned officers and employees and apply stern measures such as withholding the salaries and other emoluments of the officials/employees who continuously disregard, fail or decline to comply with the demand and stop the granting of additional cash advance. Ensure also strict compliance with COA Circulars 97-002 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, particularly the period within which to liquidate the same to avoid accumulation of unsettled cash advances.
observations.managementAction: AAR Issued final demand letter for settlement of cash advances CY 2017
observations.reason: Prior years’ cash advances remained unliquidated due to lack/loss of respective supporting documents to liquidate the cash advance.

observations.recommendation: In view of the foregoing, we reiterated previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Accountant shall prepare necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the subject cash advances to its appropriate account in accordance with the Revised Chart of Account for Local Government Units (RCALGU).
observations.managementAction: AAR Fully Implemented 2017

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management, through the Municipal Planning and Development Council Officer and Municipal Engineer to closely monitor the implementation of the proposed programs and projects. The monitoring procedure should include periodic reporting of the progress or delays in the implementation to assist the management in the performance review and in identifying gaps in implementation to allow prompt corrective action.
observations.managementAction: AAR Implement the proposed 2017 project as planned
observations.reason: Implementation was delayed due to weather conditions, beyond the insufficient target number of implementation personnel to conduct the monitoring and bulk of projects to be implemented by the LGU. (Reiterated)

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to advice the Local School Board to plan and budget the expenditures of Special Education Fund in accordance with DBM, DepEd and DILG Joint Circular No. 01, s. 2017 and Section 272 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: AAR Fully Implemented 2017

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Members of the Municipal Local School Board: a. Observe strictly the requirement set forth in Section 7 (b) of the Republic Act No. 10410 by allocating in Local School Board Budget adequate funds for various activities in support of the implementation of the ECCD Program, and comply with the guidelines on allowable charges against the Special Education Fund set forth in Section 4 of Joint Circular No. 1. s. 2017 of DepEd, DBM and DILG; and b. Conduct consultation/discussion/hearing with the local ECCD Council, Child Development Teachers, Day Care Workers and other stakeholders of the ECCD System in planning and budget preparation for the implementation of the ECCD program.
observations.managementAction: AAR Fully Implemented 2017

observations.recommendation: Management shall strictly adhere to the guidelines outlined under COA Circular 2012-002 and the requirements of RA 10121.
observations.managementAction: AAR Complied COA’s 2016 recommendation

observations.recommendation: Management should require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and update the reconciliation of the Cash in Bank accounts and give particular attention to the reconciling items. Distribute copies of the BRS to the Audit Team and the bank thru its Audit Team Leader for them to be aware of their errors and take the appropriate action of the discrepancies noted. Make proper adjustments in the books of accounts of the reconciling items for fair presentation of the Cash in Bank account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was not regularly prepared and updated the reconciliation of the cash in bank for verification due to lack of accounting staff with technical skills who can be assigned or assist the Accountant in the preparation of the BRS.
