However, BRS was prepared by the Accountant for the GF accounts only and committed to submit same to COA auditor.
observations.reason: Monthly BRS was not prepared and submitted to the Office of the Auditor for verification due to lack of accounting staff with technical skills who can be assigned or assist the Accountant in the preparation of the BRS. However, BRS was prepared by the Accountant for the GF accounts only and committed to submit same to COA auditor.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the inventory committee to conduct complete physical count
of all PPE and immediately reconcile the report with the accounting records to ensure fair
presentation the balances of PPE accounts in the financial statements.
We also recommended to prepare the RPCPPE by type to easily facilitate the
reconciliation with the accounting records. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant and
MGSO should maintain complete PPE ledger cards and property cards, respectively.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended to the Inventory Committee to conduct physical
inventory of all PPE and reconcile the results of the count with the accounting records.
Moreover, the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the GSO should gather the
relevant documents and complete details of the subject PPE items and take up the
necessary adjustments on depreciation and accumulated surplus.
Likewise, the Municipal Accountant and MGSO should maintain complete PPE ledger
cards and property cards, respectively, for each property for easy reference and reconciliation of
assets.

We recommended that the Management demand full liquidation of outstanding cash
advances from concerned officers and employees and apply stern measures such as
withholding the salaries and other emoluments of the officials/employees who
continuously disregard, fail or decline to comply with the demand and stop the granting of
additional cash advance.
Ensure also strict compliance with COA Circulars 97-002 on the grant, utilization and
liquidation of cash advances, particularly the period within which to liquidate the same to avoid
accumulation of unsettled cash advances.

In view of the foregoing, we reiterated previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal
Accountant shall prepare necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the subject cash advances to its
appropriate account in accordance with the Revised Chart of Account for Local Government Units
(RCALGU).

We recommended that the Management, through the Municipal Planning and Development
Council Officer and Municipal Engineer to closely monitor the implementation of the proposed
programs and projects. The monitoring procedure should include periodic reporting of the progress
or delays in the implementation to assist the management in the performance review and in
identifying gaps in implementation to allow prompt corrective action.

We recommended to the management to advice the Local School Board to plan and budget
the expenditures of Special Education Fund in accordance with DBM, DepEd and DILG Joint
Circular No. 01, s. 2017 and Section 272 of RA 7160.

We recommended that the Members of the Municipal Local School Board:
a. Observe strictly the requirement set forth in Section 7 (b) of the Republic Act No.
10410 by allocating in Local School Board Budget adequate funds for various
activities in support of the implementation of the ECCD Program, and comply with
the guidelines on allowable charges against the Special Education Fund set forth in
Section 4 of Joint Circular No. 1. s. 2017 of DepEd, DBM and DILG; and
b. Conduct consultation/discussion/hearing with the local ECCD Council, Child
Development Teachers, Day Care Workers and other stakeholders of the ECCD System
in planning and budget preparation for the implementation of the ECCD program.

We recommended the following:  The Municipal General Services Officer shall maintain updated and properly accomplished property cards for property accountabilities;  The Accounting Department shall establish and maintain updated PPELCs; and  The result of the physical count shall be reported in RPCPPE in accordance with the above guidelines and are promptly reconciled with accountabilities recorded in the books of accounts and the property cards and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: Inventory committee was not created to conduct the physical inventory for the ensuing year but the MGSO conducted the physical count however reconciliation with the records of the accounting was not made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make proper adjustments on the books of accounts to include all PRDP transactions for fair presentation of the affected accounts. Henceforth, the PRDP transactions shall be recorded in the computerized accounting system to avoid inadvertent omission of the said transactions.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made proper adjustments in the books of the affected transactions in the books of the affected accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following courses of action to management: a. Notify the AOs concerned for the immediate liquidation of all outstanding cash advances pursuant to COA Circulars 97-002 and 2012-004. b. Impose sanctions prescribed in the said Circulars for non-compliance thereof, such as withholding the salaries and other emoluments of the officials/employees who continuously disregard, fail or decline to comply with the demand, including filing appropriate charges against them. c. Ensure strict compliance with COA Circulars 97-002 and 2012-004 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, particularly the period within which to liquidate the same to avoid accumulation of unsettled cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Issued final demand letter
observations.reason: Prior years’ cash advances of cash remained unliquidated due to lack/loss of supporting documents to liquidate the cash advances by respective agency officer.

observations.recommendation: Municipal Accountant shall draw journal entries to transfer the amount P2,364,649.90 to its appropriate account in accordance with the revised chart of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The municipal accountant prepared necessary adjusting entries to correct the deficiency

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. Management must secure local sanggunian authority/approval for all the contracts/projects entered into/implemented by the local chief executive that were described in generic terms in the Appropriation Ordinance/AIP for CY 2016 to ratify the same and avoid disallowances pursuant to G.R. No. 175527 and COA Memorandum 2010-014. b. Henceforth, the Local Development Council and the Municipal Planning and Development Council should properly plan, prioritize and identify the specific programs/projects to be funded under the 20% EDF and incorporate the same in the AIP.
observations.managementAction: Complied COA’s recommendation.

observations.recommendation: Management shall strictly adhere to the guidelines outlined under COA Circular 2012-002 and the requirements of RA 10121.
observations.managementAction: Complied COA’s recommendation.
observations.reason: Monthly utilization report was not submitted/furnish ed to Office of the Auditor, but the Accountant and the personnel concerned committed to observe regular submission of said report to the COA pursuant to regulation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to maintain separate books of accounts for PCF to provide more effective control over the fund. Likewise, strictly adhere to the provisions of PhilHealth Circular No. 40 series of 2000 on the management of Philhealth Capitation Fund.
observations.managementAction: All agency concern must have corroborative effort; viz; for MHO to maintain logbooks of PCF vouchers payment for such transaction, bring such voucher to Accounting Office for preparation of liquidation report. The treasurer opened separate bank account for PCF for effective control.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:
The Municipal General Services Officer shall maintain updated and properly accomplished property cards for property accountabilities;
The Accounting Department shall establish and maintain updated PPELCs; and
The result of the physical count shall be reported in RPCPPE in accordance with the above guidelines and are promptly reconciled with accountabilities recorded in the books of accounts and the property cards and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make proper adjustments on the books of accounts to include all PRDP transactions for fair presentation of the affected accounts. Henceforth, the PRDP transactions shall be recorded in the computerized accounting system to avoid inadvertent omission of the said transactions.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and update the reconciliation of the Cash in Bank accounts and give particular attention to the reconciling items. Distribute copies of the BRS to the Audit Team and the bank thru its Audit Team Leader for them to be aware of their errors and take the appropriate action of the discrepancies noted. Make proper adjustments in the books of accounts of the reconciling items for fair presentation of the Cash in Bank account in the financial statements.

We recommended the following courses of action to management:
Notify the AOs concerned for the immediate liquidation of all outstanding cash advances pursuant to COA Circulars 97-002 and 2012-004.
Impose sanctions prescribed in the said Circulars for non-compliance thereof, such as withholding the salaries and other emoluments of the officials/employees who continuously disregard, fail or decline to comply with the demand, including filing appropriate charges against them.
Ensure strict compliance with COA Circulars 97-002 and 2012-004 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, particularly the period within which to liquidate the same to avoid accumulation of unsettled cash advances.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Accountant shall draw journal entries to transfer the amount P2,364,649.90 to its appropriate account in accordance with the revised chart of accounts.

We recommended that:
Management must secure local sanggunian authority/approval for all the contracts/projects entered into/implemented by the local chief executive that were described in generic terms in the Appropriation Ordinance/AIP for CY 2016 to ratify the same and avoid disallowances pursuant to G.R. No. 175527 and COA Memorandum 2010-014.
Henceforth, the Local Development Council and the Municipal Planning and Development Council should properly plan, prioritize and identify the specific programs/projects to be funded under the 20% EDF and incorporate the same in the AIP.
We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that management shall strictly adhere to the guidelines outlined under COA Circular 2012-002 and the requirements of RA 10121.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to maintain separate books of accounts for PCF to provide more effective control over the fund. Likewise, strictly adhere to the provisions of PhilHealth Circular No. 40 series of 2000 on the management of Philhealth Capitation Fund.
